SEARCH
Package neqsim.process.safety.reaction

Class RunawayReactionResult

java.lang.Object
neqsim.process.safety.reaction.RunawayReactionResult
All Implemented Interfaces:
Serializable
public class RunawayReactionResult extends Object implements Serializable
Immutable result of a runaway (exothermic) reaction excursion screening.

Captures the adiabatic temperature rise, the maximum temperature of the synthesis reaction (MTSR), the margin to a supplied maximum-allowable temperature, an optional adiabatic time-to-maximum-rate (TMR), and a screening verdict. The result is a screening artefact intended to flag scenarios that require a full reaction-calorimetry study and DIERS two-phase relief design; it is not a finished thermal-hazard assessment.

Version:
1.0
Author:
ESOL
See Also:

  • Constructor Details

    • RunawayReactionResult

      public RunawayReactionResult(double processTemperatureK, double adiabaticTemperatureRiseK, double mtsrK, double maxAllowableTemperatureK, double temperatureMarginK, boolean maxAllowableProvided, boolean marginExceeded, double totalHeatReleaseJ, double totalMassKg, double specificHeatJPerKgK, boolean timeToMaxRateProvided, double timeToMaxRateS, boolean twoPhaseReliefScreeningRequired, RunawayReactionAnalyzer.RunawayVerdict verdict, List<String> warnings)
      Construct an immutable runaway-reaction screening result.
      Parameters:
      processTemperatureK - initial process temperature [K]
      adiabaticTemperatureRiseK - adiabatic temperature rise dT_ad [K]
      mtsrK - maximum temperature of the synthesis reaction (adiabatic) [K]
      maxAllowableTemperatureK - maximum-allowable temperature (decomposition onset or material limit) [K], NaN if not supplied
      temperatureMarginK - margin = MAT - MTSR [K], NaN if no MAT supplied
      maxAllowableProvided - true if a maximum-allowable temperature was supplied
      marginExceeded - true if MTSR exceeds the maximum-allowable temperature
      totalHeatReleaseJ - total adiabatic heat release [J]
      totalMassKg - total reacting mass [kg]
      specificHeatJPerKgK - specific heat used [J/(kg·K)]
      timeToMaxRateProvided - true if an adiabatic TMR was computed
      timeToMaxRateS - adiabatic time-to-maximum-rate [s], NaN if not computable
      twoPhaseReliefScreeningRequired - true if DIERS two-phase relief screening is recommended
      verdict - the screening verdict
      warnings - non-fatal warnings raised during analysis

      non-fatal warnings raised during analysis

    • toJson

      public String toJson()
      Serialise this result to pretty-printed JSON. Special floating-point values (NaN for an absent margin or TMR) are permitted so that a result without a temperature rating still serialises.
      Returns:
      JSON representation of this result
